only-md.description: Allows writes and edits only to *.md files.

no-env-access.description: Blocks reads and writes to .env files.

paths-in-cwd.description: Blocks read/write/edit whose .path resolves outside the project cwd. Resolves with python3 os.path.realpath on both the path and PI_CWD (joined when relative), so it handles ../, ./, absolute paths, and double slashes, AND follows symlinks — a symlink inside cwd that points outside is blocked too. Requires python3 (stdlib only, no pip). Fails closed (blocks) if python3 is missing or errors. Empty or missing .path is allowed (nothing to escape).
paths-in-cwd.shell: python3 -c 'import os,sys; d=__import__("json").loads(os.environ["PI_TOOL_INPUT"]); p=d.get("path",""); c=os.path.realpath(os.environ["PI_CWD"]); r=os.path.realpath(p if os.path.isabs(p) else os.path.join(c,p)); sys.exit(0 if (not p or r==c or r.startswith(c+os.sep)) else 1)' || exit 1

read-max-500-chars.description: Blocks read results exceeding 500 characters (approx. 125 tokens).

read-max-10000-chars.description: Blocks read results exceeding 10,000 characters (approx. 2,500 tokens).

read-max-20000-chars.description: Blocks read results exceeding 20,000 characters (approx. 5,000 tokens).

read-max-50000-chars.description: Blocks read results exceeding 50,000 characters (approx. 12,500 tokens).

read-max-100000-chars.description: Blocks read results exceeding 100,000 characters (approx. 25,000 tokens).

read-max-200000-chars.description: Blocks read results exceeding 200,000 characters (approx. 50,000 tokens).

read-max-500000-chars.description: Blocks read results exceeding 500,000 characters (approx. 125,000 tokens).

no-env-secrets-in-output.description: Blocks tool results that leak a secret read from .env. Parses .env at the project root, collecting values of vars whose name contains 'key' or 'secret' (case-insensitive, substring match), and blocks (exit 1) if any such value of length >= 8 appears in PI_TOOL_RESULT. The length floor skips trivial flag values like DEBUG_KEY=true. No-ops (allow) when .env is absent (when guard) or when no qualifying secrets are found. Uses grep -F (fixed-string) so values containing regex metacharacters are matched literally.
no-env-secrets-in-output.shell: T=$(mktemp)||exit 0; awk 'BEGIN{D=sprintf("%c",34);S=sprintf("%c",39)} {l=$0; sub(/^[[:space:]]+/,"",l); if(l==""||substr(l,1,1)=="#")next; sub(/^export[[:space:]]+/,"",l); eq=index(l,"="); if(eq==0)next; k=tolower(substr(l,1,eq-1)); v=substr(l,eq+1); sub(/^[[:space:]]+/,"",v); sub(/[[:space:]]+$/,"",v); if(substr(v,1,1)==D&&substr(v,length(v),1)==D)v=substr(v,2,length(v)-2); else if(substr(v,1,1)==S&&substr(v,length(v),1)==S)v=substr(v,2,length(v)-2); if((index(k,"key")>0||index(k,"secret")>0)&&length(v)>=8)print v }' .env >"$T"; if [ -s "$T" ]&&printf '%s' "$PI_TOOL_RESULT"|grep -Fq -f "$T"; then r=1; else r=0; fi; rm -f "$T"; exit $r
